title: Anthropic Introduces Claude Opus 4 and Sonnet 4, Sets New Coding Benchmarks
summary: Anthropic released Claude Opus 4 and Claude Sonnet 4, its most capable models yet, with Opus 4 claiming the title of world's best coding model and both supporting hybrid reasoning with extended tool use.
content: Anthropic introduced Claude Opus 4 and Claude Sonnet 4, setting new standards for coding, reasoning, and AI agent workflows. Claude Opus 4 leads on SWE-bench with a 72.5% score and Terminal-bench at 43.2%, capable of running continuously for several hours on complex, multi-thousand-step tasks. Both models are hybrid, offering near-instant responses and an extended thinking mode that can now invoke tools like web search during the reasoning process. Alongside the models, Anthropic announced Claude Code is now generally available, with new VS Code and JetBrains integrations and a Claude Code SDK for building custom agents. Additional API capabilities include a code execution tool, MCP connector, Files API, and one-hour prompt caching—all designed to make it easier for developers to build powerful multi-step agents.

title: US House Passes 10-Year Moratorium on State AI Regulation
summary: The US House voted 215–214 to prohibit states from enforcing AI-related laws for 10 years as part of its budget reconciliation bill, igniting a fierce debate over federal preemption of state consumer protections.
content: On May 22, 2025, the US House of Representatives passed a budget reconciliation bill containing a sweeping 10-year moratorium that would bar states and localities from enforcing laws or regulations governing AI models, AI systems, or automated decision systems. The provision could halt enforcement of hundreds of state laws addressing deepfakes, algorithmic discrimination, online safety, and age verification—covering more than 1,000 AI-related bills introduced across states in 2025 alone. Republicans backing the measure argued it prevents a damaging regulatory patchwork, while Democrats warned it strips protections during a period of rapid AI deployment. Senator Ted Cruz has separately indicated plans to pursue similar federal preemption through standalone legislation. The bill now heads to the Senate, where the moratorium faces potential procedural challenges under the Byrd Rule, which restricts non-budgetary provisions in reconciliation bills.

title: Google Rolls Out AI Mode to All US Users, Signals Total Reinvention of Search
summary: Google announced at I/O 2025 that AI Mode—a Gemini-powered chatbot built into Search—is now available to all US users, with agentic features like Deep Search and Project Mariner that can browse and act on your behalf.
content: At Google I/O on May 20, Google announced it is rolling out AI Mode to all US users, transforming Google Search with a Gemini-powered experience that can reason, synthesize, and take action rather than just retrieve links. New features include Deep Search, which conducts multiple searches and synthesizes broad topic summaries; Project Mariner, which can browse the web and complete tasks like booking travel or buying concert tickets autonomously; and Search Live, which lets users interact via conversation and camera. AI Mode can also access users' prior search history and optionally connect to Gmail for personalized context. Google revealed that 1.5 billion people now see AI Overviews every month, and executives described the traditional search results page as a historical construct that AI will replace with dynamically generated content—including videos, charts, and custom web apps—tailored to each query. Google's head of search said the product will be unrecognizable in three years.

title: Microsoft Introduces Copilot Tuning and Multi-Agent Orchestration at Build 2025
summary: Microsoft unveiled Copilot Tuning at Build 2025, a low-code tool letting organizations train AI models on their own company data, alongside multi-agent orchestration that lets Copilot Studio agents collaborate as a team.
content: At Microsoft Build 2025, Microsoft announced Copilot Tuning, a low-code capability in Copilot Studio that lets any organization train AI models using their own data, workflows, and processes—no data scientists required. A legal firm, for example, can create an agent that drafts arguments in its institutional style, while a consulting company can tune agents for specific industry expertise, all within the Microsoft 365 security boundary. Microsoft also introduced multi-agent orchestration, enabling specialized agents across HR, IT, and marketing to collaborate on complex tasks like onboarding new employees. The Copilot Wave 2 spring release moved to general availability simultaneously, adding a new Copilot app, Create experience, and Copilot Notebooks. Over 230,000 organizations—including 90% of the Fortune 500—now use Copilot Studio, and customers created more than one million custom agents last quarter, a 130% jump quarter-over-quarter.

title: OpenAI Launches Codex, Its Most Capable AI Coding Agent
summary: OpenAI launched a research preview of Codex, a cloud-based AI coding agent powered by a version of o3 that can write features, fix bugs, and run tests autonomously across multiple tasks simultaneously.
content: OpenAI launched a research preview of Codex, its most capable AI coding agent yet, powered by codex-1—a version of the o3 reasoning model optimized for software engineering tasks that produces cleaner code and iteratively runs tests until they pass. The agent runs in a sandboxed cloud environment that connects to GitHub to preload code repositories, handling tasks from writing new features to fixing bugs and answering codebase questions—each taking between one and 30 minutes to complete. Codex can work on multiple engineering tasks simultaneously without blocking users from accessing their own computers, and operates in an air-gapped environment with no access to the broader internet. Rolling out first to ChatGPT Pro, Enterprise, and Team subscribers, it enters a booming AI coding market where Cursor recently reached a $9 billion valuation and OpenAI has reportedly agreed to acquire Windsurf for $3 billion. OpenAI's agents research lead described the long-term vision as AI coding agents becoming 'virtual teammates' capable of completing tasks that take human engineers hours or days.

title: Google DeepMind's AlphaEvolve Invents New Algorithms, Beats 56-Year-Old Math Solution
summary: Google DeepMind unveiled AlphaEvolve, a Gemini-powered AI agent that discovers and optimizes algorithms, already improving Google's data center efficiency by 0.7% and surpassing a matrix multiplication method from 1969.
content: Google DeepMind unveiled AlphaEvolve, an evolutionary coding agent built on Gemini that uses an automated evaluation framework to generate, score, and iteratively improve algorithms across mathematical and real-world problems. The system uses Gemini Flash for breadth of ideas and Gemini Pro for depth, with each candidate solution verified by an automatic evaluator—a design that sidesteps the hallucination problems of standard chatbots. AlphaEvolve has already been deployed across Google's global data centers, where it improved scheduling heuristics to free up 0.7% of total computing resources, a significant gain at Google's scale. It also discovered a new algorithm for multiplying 4×4 complex-valued matrices using 48 scalar multiplications, improving on Volker Strassen's 1969 method that had stood as the best known approach for decades. Tested on more than 50 open mathematical problems, AlphaEvolve matched the best existing solutions in 75% of cases and found strictly better solutions in 20% of cases.
